topic.what: Reverse-engineering and fuzzing of Apple's undocumented ARI baseband protocol on iOS
topic.how: Ghidra scripts to auto-generate Wireshark dissector from iOS shared libraries; in-process fuzzing via Frida targeting CommCenter
topic.why_matters: Zero-click RCE attack surface via wireless baseband; uncovered 42 crashes indicating lack of prior security testing by Apple

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of unique crashes found in CommCenter and related daemons
characteristics.context: 42 crashes in CommCenter affecting 12 daemons via XPC communication

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of iOS versions where automated dissector generation worked
characteristics.context: Spans iOS 12.5.1 through 14.5 with consistent extraction

characteristics.what_itasures: Fuzz cases per second achievable with local high-speed mutation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Accuracy of automated message type classification via nemetyl
characteristics.context: 30 of 32 clusters contained messages from exactly one group

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of message groups and types in ARI protocol
characteristics.context: iOS 14.5 with 783 total types across all definitions

characteristics.what_itasures: iOS versions needed to fix discovered vulnerabilities
characteristics.context: All replayable crashes fixed by iOS 14.6; misd bug fixed in 14.2

novelty: First public reverse-engineering of ARI protocol
Ghidra scripts auto-generating Wireshark dissectors from closed-source iOS libraries
In-process Frida fuzzing of CommCenter via ARI interface
Cross-version protocol tracking across 10 iOS releases
Automated TLV and type definition extraction from shared cache
open_problems: Static traffic analysis requires further optimization for bit-level non-continuous fields
Coverage-based fuzzing suffers from inconsistent coverage due to protocol state dependency
ARM decompiler inaccuracies complicate crash analysis
Limited iOS emulation support for cellular daemons
No CVE assignment despite security-critical findings
